ukm:Metadata.dc:title: Finance Act 2003
ukm:Metadata.dc:description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with finance.

ukm:UnappliedEffect.Notes: This amendment not applied to legislation.gov.uk. The affecting S.I. is revoked and superseded by S.I. 2003/2816
ukm:UnappliedEffect.ukm:AffectingTitle: The Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Land Tax (Variation of the Finance Act 2003) Regulations 2003

ukm:UnappliedEffect.Comments: TOES: Effect is superseded by the repeal of Sch. 12 para. 3 by Finance Act 2008 (c. 9), s. 129(4), Sch. 43 para. 16 - TOES: Effect is superseded by the repeal of Sch. 12 para. 3 by Finance Act 2008 (c. 9), s. 129(4), Sch. 43 para. 16 - TOES: Effect is superseded by the repeal of Sch. 12 para. 3 by Finance Act 2008 (c. 9), s. 129(4), Sch. 43 para. 16
ukm:UnappliedEffect.Notes: This effect was superseded by the repeal of Sch. 12 para. 3 by Finance Act 2008 (c. 9), s. 129(4), Sch. 43 para. 16

ukm:InForce.AppendedCommentary: (which transitional provisions in art. 4 are revoked (31.1.2011) by S.S.I. 2011/31, art. 5(c))

ukm:UnappliedEffect.Notes: The amending provision was repealed before coming into force.
ukm:UnappliedEffect.Comments: TOES - Amending provision repealed before coming into force. - TOES - Amending provision repealed before coming into force. - TOES - Amending provision repealed before coming into force.

ukm:UnappliedEffect.Notes: The amending provision was repealed before coming into force.
ukm:UnappliedEffect.Comments: TOES - Amending provision repealed before coming into force. - TOES - Amending provision repealed before coming into force. - TOES - Amending provision repealed before coming into force.
This effect has been added as an appended commentary in the relevant effects in the TOES for 2018 anaw 1. No further action required.
ukm:UnappliedEffect.Type: savings for amendments by 2018 anaw 1, s. 6, Sch. 6
ukm:UnappliedEffect.TypeNotes: savings for amendments by 2018 anaw 1, s. 6, Sch. 6
ukm:UnappliedEffect.ukm:AffectingTitle: The Abolition of the Right to Buy and Associated Rights (Wales) Act 2018 (Consequential Amendments and Savings Provisions) Regulations 2019
Rheoliadau Deddf Diddymu’r Hawl i Brynu a Hawliau Cysylltiedig (Cymru) 2018 (Diwygiadau Canlyniadol a Darpariaethau Arbed) 2019

Text.Addition: Relief may be claimed for a chargeable transaction if the following conditions are met (but this is subject to sub-paragraph (7)).
The first condition is that the main subject-matter of the transaction consists of a major interest in a single dwelling (“the purchased dwelling”).
The second condition is that the relevant consideration for the transaction (other than any consisting of rent) is not more than £500,000.
The third condition is that the purchaser, or (if more than one) each of the purchasers, is a first-time buyer who intends to occupy the purchased dwelling as the purchaser's only or main residence.
The fourth condition is that—
the transaction is not linked to another land transaction, or
the transaction is linked only to land transactions that are within sub-paragraph (6).
A land transaction is within this sub-paragraph if the main subject-matter of the transaction consists of—
an interest in land that is or forms part of the garden or grounds of the purchased dwelling, or
an interest in or right over land that subsists for the benefit of—
the purchased dwelling, or
land that is or forms part of the garden or grounds of the purchased dwelling.
Relief may not be claimed under this paragraph for a chargeable transaction if it is a higher rates transaction for the purposes of paragraph 1 of Schedule 4ZA.

Emphasis.Addition: Eligibility for relief: linked transactions within paragraph 1(6)

Text.Addition: Where a land transaction (“the main transaction”) is eligible for relief under paragraph 1 (or would be if it were a chargeable transaction), relief may also be claimed for any chargeable transaction that is linked to the main transaction.

But relief may not be claimed under this paragraph for a chargeable transaction if the purchaser, or (if more than one) any of the purchasers in relation to the transaction is not a purchaser in relation to the main transaction.

Emphasis.Addition: Eligibility for relief: alternative finance arrangements

Text.Addition: This paragraph applies in relation to a land transaction which is the first transaction under an alternative finance arrangement entered into between a person and a financial institution.
The person (rather than the institution) is to be treated as the purchaser in relation to the transaction for the purposes of paragraphs 1(4) and 2(2).
In this paragraph—
” means an arrangement of a kind mentioned in section 71A(1) or 73(1),
” has the meaning it has in those sections (see section 73BA), and
”, in relation to an alternative finance arrangement, has the meaning given by section 71A(1)(a) or (as the case may be) section 73(1)(a)(i).

Emphasis.Addition: Eligibility for relief: acquisition of new lease on bare trust

Text.Addition: In determining who is the purchaser for the purposes of paragraphs 1(4) and 2(2), paragraph 3 of Schedule 16 is to have effect as if sub-paragraphs (2) and (3) (trustee of bare trust granted a lease treated as purchaser of the whole of the interest acquired) were omitted.

If relief is claimed under paragraph 1 or 2 for a chargeable transaction, the amount of tax chargeable in respect of the transaction is to be determined as if in section 55(1B) (amount of tax chargeable: general) for Table A there were substituted—

xhtml:td.Addition: So much as does not exceed £300,000
Any remainder (so far as not exceeding £500,000)

Text.Addition: This paragraph applies if—
relief is claimed under paragraph 1 or 2 for a chargeable transaction (“the first transaction”), and

the effect of another land transaction (“the later transaction”) that is linked to the first transaction is that the first transaction ceases to be a transaction for which relief may be claimed under that paragraph.
Tax or (as the case may be) additional tax is chargeable on the first transaction as if the claim had not been made.
In this Schedule “
” means an individual who—
has not previously been a purchaser in relation to a land transaction the main subject-matter of which was a major interest in a dwelling,
has not previously acquired
an equivalent interest in a dwelling situated in a country or territory outside England, Wales and Northern Ireland,

Addition.Addition: an interest of a kind mentioned in section 117(2) in a dwelling situated in Wales,

Text.Addition: has not previously been, or been one of the persons who was, “
” for the purposes of section 71A or 73 in a case where the main subject-matter of the first transaction within the meaning of the section concerned was a major interest in a dwelling, and

would not have been such a person for those purposes in such a case if the provisions mentioned in paragraph (c) had been in force, and had had effect in the country or territory concerned at all material times (subject, where required, to appropriate modifications).
For the purposes of sub-paragraph (1)(b) and (d), ignore a lease

Addition.Addition: or, in the case of a dwelling situated in Wales, a term of years absolute

Text.Addition: which has less than 21 years to run at the beginning of the day after the date on which it is acquired.

In determining any question for the purposes of sub-paragraph (1), paragraph 3 of Schedule 16 is to have effect as if sub-paragraphs (2) and (3) (trustee of bare trust granted a lease treated as purchaser of the whole of the interest acquired) were omitted.
In this Schedule “
” means—
in the case of a transaction that is not one of a number of linked transactions, the chargeable consideration for the transaction, and
in the case of a transaction that is one of a number of linked transactions, the total of the chargeable consideration for all those transactions.

The main subject-matter of a transaction is not a major interest for the purposes of this Schedule if it is a term of years absolute which has less than 21 years to run at the beginning of the day after the effective date of the transaction.
This paragraph sets out rules for determining what counts as a dwelling for the purposes of this Schedule.
A building or part of a building counts as a dwelling if—
it is used or suitable for use as a single dwelling, or
it is in the process of being constructed or adapted for such use.
Land that is, or is to be, occupied or enjoyed with a dwelling as a garden or grounds (including any building or structure on that land) is taken to be part of that dwelling.
Land that subsists, or is to subsist, for the benefit of a dwelling is taken to be part of that dwelling.
The main subject-matter of a transaction is also taken to consist of a major interest in a dwelling if—
substantial performance of a contract constitutes the effective date of that transaction by virtue of a relevant deeming provision,

the main subject-matter of the transaction consists of a major interest in a building, or a part of a building, that is to be constructed or adapted under the contract for use as a single dwelling, and
construction or adaptation of the building, or part of a building, has not begun by the time the contract is substantially performed.
In sub-paragraph (5)—
” includes any agreement,
” means any of sections 44 to 45A or paragraph 5(1) or (2) of Schedule 2A or paragraph 12 of Schedule 17A, and
” has the same meaning as in section 44.
A building or part of a building used for a purpose specified in section 116(2) or (3) is not used as a dwelling for the purposes of sub-paragraphs (2) or (5).
Where a building or part of a building is used for a purpose mentioned in sub-paragraph (7), no account is to be taken for the purposes of sub-paragraph (2) of its suitability for any other use.

Para.Text: Sch. 6ZA inserted (with effect in accordance with s. 41(8) of the amending Act) by Finance Act 2018 (c. 3), s. 41(3)
Words in Sch. 6ZA para. 6(2) inserted (1.4.2018 with effect in accordance with s. 16(4)(5) of the amending Act) by Wales Act 2014 (c. 29), s. 29(2)(b)(3), Sch. 2 para. 9A(3); S.I. 2018/214, art. 2(a)

Words in Sch. 6ZA para. 6(1)(b) inserted (1.4.2018 with effect in accordance with s. 16(4)(5) of the amending Act) by Wales Act 2014 (c. 29), s. 29(2)(b)(3), Sch. 2 para. 9A(2)(a); S.I. 2018/214, art. 2(a)

Words in Sch. 6ZA para. 6(1)(b) inserted (1.4.2018 with effect in accordance with s. 16(4)(5) of the amending Act) by Wales Act 2014 (c. 29), s. 29(2)(b)(3), Sch. 2 para. 9A(2)(b); S.I. 2018/214, art. 2(a)
Sch. 6ZA modified (temp.) (8.2.2023) by Stamp Duty Land Tax (Temporary Relief) Act 2023 (c. 2), s. 1
Sch. 6ZA para. 3A and cross-heading inserted (with effect in accordance with s. 8(4) of the amending Act) by Finance (No. 2) Act 2024 (c. 12), s. 8(2)
Sch. 6ZA para. 6(3) inserted (with effect in accordance with s. 8(5)(6) of the amending Act) by Finance (No. 2) Act 2024 (c. 12), s. 8(3)
